summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that further split the SysDolphin region into mostly named component files and fixed ldscript ordering/naming so the linker still matched. The PR extracted code from broad address-named asm blobs into files such as gobj, gobjproc, gobjplink, gobjgxlink, gobjuserdata, psappsrt, psdisptev_, sobj, and sislib, while retaining residual address-named files code_80391580 and code_803A949C. It also renamed some files to their more correct names, using a trailing underscore where the natural object name caused linker/name matching conflicts.
what_changed: Split most of the SysDolphin assembly out of large generic address-named files into smaller, system-named asm files.
Renamed asm/code_8038FAA8.s to asm/code_80391580.s and removed the earlier extracted portion, leaving the later residual code starting around func_80391580.
Renamed asm/code_803A3920.s to asm/code_803A949C.s and removed the extracted portion, leaving residual code around lbl_803A949C.
Added new asm files for SysDolphin-related components: gobj.s, gobjproc.s, gobjplink.s, gobjgxlink.s, gobjobject_.s, gobjuserdata.s, psappsrt.s, psdisptev_.s, sobj.s, and sislib.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf to replace the old broad object entries code_8038FAA8.o and code_803A3920.o with the newly split object sequence while preserving link order.

Renamed strng.o to string_.o, dsp_dbg.o to dsp_debug.o, and debug.o to debug_.o in ldscript.lcf; the diff also shows corresponding 100% asm file renames for strng.s to string_.s, dsp_dbg.s to dsp_debug.s, and debug.s to debug_.s.
Used trailing underscores for files where the intended source/object name caused a matching or linker naming issue, visible in gobjobject_.s, psdisptev_.s, debug_.s, and string_.s.
smart_moves: Kept the work as an assembly split rather than attempting functional rewrites, making the PR primarily about organization and linker correctness.
Preserved exact object order in ldscript.lcf by replacing each old monolithic object with a sequence of split objects at the same location in the text section.
Used meaningful subsystem names for files once enough of the SysDolphin region was understood, improving future searchability and decomp targeting.
Left address-named residual files for unsplit/unknown tail regions instead of over-naming uncertain code.
Adopted a simple trailing-underscore convention for files whose correct names conflicted with linker/matching constraints, allowing accurate names without breaking the build.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large asm blob, update the linker script at the same time and treat object order as part of the matching contract.
A partial split can still be valuable: move understood ranges into named files and leave the remaining unknown ranges under address-based names.
Source/object file names can affect linker matching; filename cleanup is not purely cosmetic in this repository.
Use a trailing underscore as a practical escape hatch when the desired canonical name causes a linker/name collision or other matching issue.
Large subsystem splits should be evidence-driven: this PR's body only claims SysDolphin is nearly entirely named, not that the new names are fully documented in headers or C code.
100% similarity renames in asm, such as debug.s to debug_.s or strng.s to string_.s, can be linker fixups rather than code changes.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem/component names for SysDolphin asm files where known: gobj, gobjproc, gobjplink, gobjgxlink, gobjuserdata, psappsrt, psdisptev, sobj, sislib.
Use address-based filenames such as code_80391580.s and code_803A949C.s for remaining unknown or unsplit address ranges.
Append a trailing underscore to the filename/object name when the correct name would cause a matching issue: string_, debug_, gobjobject_, psdisptev_.
Rename abbreviated or placeholder names toward fuller intended names where possible, shown by dsp_dbg becoming dsp_debug.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split monolithic asm into multiple .s files without changing instruction bodies, relying on ldscript.lcf to preserve the final linked layout.
Replace one old object entry with several new object entries in the exact region where the original object appeared.
Keep residual address-named objects after extracted named chunks so unresolved code can remain linked in the correct position.
Use 100% similarity file renames for object-name/linker fixups where no instruction-level changes are intended.
Use underscore-suffixed object names in ldscript.lcf to avoid linker matching problems while preserving recognizable source names.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: How were gobjproc, gobjplink, gobjgxlink, gobjobject_, and gobjuserdata later decompiled into C?
Were the underscore-suffixed files debug_, string_, psdisptev_, and gobjobject_ later renamed or resolved?
Which later PR introduced headers or structs for the SysDolphin gobj/sobj/sislib regions split in PR #25?
Did ldscript.lcf ordering around AXDriver, gobj, psdisp, psappsrt, sobj, and sislib remain stable after this split?
What exact linker/name collisions motivated the trailing underscore convention in this PR?
